posts.author: DavisSmith (Davis Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

We manage SEO for a fairly large eCommerce catalog, and coordinating updates across product pages, category pages, and technical fixes was getting difficult. Recently we started organizing everything in monday.com using separate boards for keyword updates, on-page fixes, and technical SEO tasks.

One challenge is aligning SEO priorities with content and merchandising teams when hundreds of SKUs are involved. While structuring our workflow, we also reviewed some approaches around ecommerce SEO services to better organize category optimization and product-page improvements.

Thanks!

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
It sounds like you’ve already started structuring things with separate boards for keyword updates, on-page fixes, and technical SEO tasks — that’s a solid starting point for large catalogs.

Out of curiosity, are you looking for feedback on the structure, or how others manage SEO priorities across hundreds of SKUs in monday.com?

One pattern I’ve seen teams use is separating SEO initiatives from the product catalog and then linking them with Connect Boards. That way category-level optimizations or technical fixes can roll up across many SKUs without creating hundreds of duplicate tasks.

If you can share a bit more about where the friction is (prioritization, reporting, or cross-team coordination), the community might be able to suggest a few approaches.
